source.title: Targeting insulo-frontal pathway to reduce stress-evoked cognitive rigidity

confidence_rationale: Provides direct optogenetic causal evidence that aIC → mPFC pathway signals incorrect trial outcomes, elevates mPFC updating on subsequent trials, and restores adaptive mode-switching in stressed animals. This is the closest available empirical paper to the SD-032c urgency-interrupt hypothesis: aIC detects salient negative events and fires a control signal to prefrontal cortex that triggers behavioral mode revision. Confidence moderated by preprint status and the fact the study addresses cognitive set-shifting rather than harm-salience urgency per se.
failure_signatures: If aIC signals all outcome valences (not specifically salient negative events), its role is general prediction-error reporting rather than urgency interrupt.
If the pathway effect is on working memory updating rather than network mode switching, SD-032c may need to be reframed as a PFC-update signal rather than a mode-switch trigger.

mapping.source_claim_statement: aIC neurons projecting to mPFC show heightened activity following incorrect (salient negative) trials; this elevated activity persists and provides a salience signal enhancing mPFC outcome-dependent updating. Optogenetic reinforcement of aIC→mPFC after incorrect trials restores cognitive flexibility in stressed mice.
mapping.ree_translation: SD-032c proposes that AICAnalog fires a switch trigger to the SalienceCoordinator (SD-032a) when z_harm_a exceeds a threshold. Ma et al. provide causal optogenetic evidence that aIC → prefrontal signalling after salient negative events IS the mechanism for behavioural mode revision, directly supporting the interrupt-trigger architecture.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper uses cognitive set-shifting (attentional set-shifting task) as the mode-switch proxy, not harm-salience urgency or homeostatic drive modulation. The drive_level dependence (SD-032c's key testable specification) is not addressed. Preprint status adds additional uncertainty.
mapping.source_context: Mouse optogenetics, attentional set-shifting task (AST), two-photon calcium imaging of aIC → mPFC projections, stress model.

confidence_components.notes: bioRxiv preprint 2026, Zuo lab. Strong optogenetic design but preprint, and task maps to cognitive flexibility not urgency interrupt. Mapping fidelity good but not tight.
